title: Extrapolating
alt: By the third trimester, there will be hundreds of babies inside you.
transcript:
My Hobby: Extrapolating

[There is a graph. Time runs along the horizontal axis; Number of Husbands on the vertical graph. Yesterday and today are labeled in time, 0 and 1 in number of husbands. Points are plotted with 0 at yesterday, 1 at today. A straight line is fitted through them, and dotted lines joining perpendicularily the points to the axes.]
[Cueball is holding a pointer to the graph, and looking at Megan wearing a bridal train and veil, who seems to be thinking.]
Cueball: As you can see, by late next month you'll have over four dozen husbands.
Cueball: Better get a bulk rate on wedding cake.
explanation:
This comic is a joke about the incorrect application of . Extrapolation is a form of estimation in which new data points are estimated from existing ones using some method. In this case, Cueball's method is extending a straight line through existing data points, which is linear extrapolation. This method works if the relationship being modelled is also linear - however, weddings are usually not.

By connecting two points without any context in this manner, we can easily come up with funny and absurd results. Here, connecting a bride's number of spouses yesterday (zero) and today—her wedding day—(one) results in a linear extrapolation to hundreds of spouses a year, which would also require the purchase of many wedding cakes. Cueball seems to believe this accumulation of husbands is a phenomenon beyond the bride's ability to control. Using similar data points for pregnancy (yesterday: no babies, today: one), we can get 200+ children inside a single person by the seventh month of pregnancy.

This is another comic in the My Hobby series. Extrapolation and interpolation (estimating data points between two existing ones) are also recurrent topics on xkcd.
